cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to find out what Jens Kristensen decided to do when the earthquake struck. The passage says: "In a split second, I considered whether to run for the door or hide under my desk," says Kristensen, 48. "The door was closed, and I thought that maybe it was too far and I would be caught under falling debris, so I hid under the table." So, when the earthquake hit, he had two options: run for the door or hide under the desk. He thought the door was too far and that he might get caught in falling debris, so he decided to hide under the table. Looking at the options: (A) read documents in the office - This was what he was doing before the earthquake, not what he decided to do during it. (B) stay under the desk - This matches what he decided to do, according to his own words. (C) run out of the room quickly - He considered this, but decided against it because the door was closed.
